strengths: 15+ years of full-stack TypeScript/Node.js experience with demonstrated production delivery across multiple senior and staff roles
AWS exposure (CDK at Optilyz) and Kubernetes usage (GISCollective) partially satisfy cloud/infra nice-to-haves
Active AI-agent and MCP framework work at re:cinq signals strong learning agility and current technical relevance
concerns: PostgreSQL is entirely absent from the resume; primary database experience is MongoDB (NoSQL), which is a direct gap against the preferred core requirement
NestJS is not mentioned anywhere; React appears only once and in a supporting role, leaving both key framework requirements undersubstantiated

The role is junior/mid-level individual contributor with high on-site presence; a 15+ year Staff-level professional is a severe level and culture mismatch likely to create retention risk and compensation conflict
response.justification: This candidate's profile is fundamentally misaligned with the role on the two most disqualifying axes: seniority level and stack fit. A Staff/Senior engineer with 15+ years of experience applying for an entry-level individual contributor position creates an almost certain retention and compensation mismatch — the role will not challenge or adequately compensate this candidate, and attrition risk is high. On the technical side, PostgreSQL (the preferred database) does not appear anywhere in the resume, primary database experience is MongoDB, and NestJS — one of two core framework requirements — is entirely missing. React exposure is a single line at one employer. The industry background (ad-tech, direct mail, fintech, geospatial) has no overlap with renewable energy or construction technology, removing any domain knowledge upside that might offset the stack gaps. Taken together, the candidate is overqualified in experience level but underqualified on the specific stack this role actually needs.
